channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michigan Democratic Sen. Gary Peters will not run for reelection, opening a key Senate seat in 2026
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Sen. Gary Peters, who led the Democrats’ Senate campaign efforts the past two election cycles, has announced he will not seek a third term in 2026, creating a highly contested battleground seat expected to be coveted by both major political parties.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First on Fox: Trump Cabinet nominee Loeffler pledges to donate salary to charity if confirmed
item.description: Former Sen. Kelly Loeffler, President Donald Trump's nominee to lead the Small Business Administration, plans to donate her federal salary to charity, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Trump voters are skeptical of his opening moves to embrace fellow billionaires
item.description: Some of President Donald Trump's working-class and middle-class supporters see a lack of emphasis on lowering consumer costs and making daily American life more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Bill Barrow And Amelia Thomson Deveaux, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump admin pauses federal grants, demands return to office details in memo blitz
item.description: The Trump administration OPM and OMB offices went on a memo blitz on Monday, including directing agency leaders to pause federal grants and to deliver return to office plans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deb Haaland made history as Interior secretary. Now she’s running for governor of New Mexico
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Deb Haaland, who championed conservation and clean energy during her tenure as Interior secretary, is running for the Democratic nomination for governor in New Mexico, the nation's No. 2 oil production state.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gary Peters, Democratic senator from Trump state, won't seek re-election
item.description: Michigan Democratic Senator Gary Peters will not seek re-election to a third term when his current one expires in January 2027, leaving open a senate seat in a state Donald Trump won.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DC Councilmember Trayon White faces expulsion hearing over federal bribery charge
item.description: The D.C. Council is holding an expulsion hearing today for Trayon White, a member accused by the Justice Department of accepting $156,000 in bribes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Karoline Leavitt, the youngest White House press secretary, makes her debut in the briefing room
item.description: The youngest person to serve as White House press secretary has made her debut in the briefing room, promising to hold briefings often and saying President Donald Trump also will be available to reporters for questions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: K-12 education and the Minnesota Legislature: 5 things to watch this session
item.description: Even as Minnesota lawmakers struggle to start their new session, education committee leaders in the House and Senate are quietly starting to set budget and policy priorities. Here’s a look at what might be coming.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Johnson opens door to linking voter ID to California disaster aid after Trump demand
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is keeping the door open to conditioning California disaster aid on the state enacting voter identification laws, days after President Trump said he wanted to link the two priorities.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Republicans end DeSantis special session before calling their own
item.description: Lawmakers in Tallahassee on Monday abruptly ended a special session called by Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis to address illegal immigration and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump huddles with House GOP at retreat in Florida
item.description: GOP leaders will try to move Trump’s legislative agenda, including an extension of 2017 tax cuts and border policy.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Investor seeks to quash U.S. Steel-Nippon deal after taking stake in U.S. steelmaker
item.description: An asset manager is seeking to quash Nippon Steel’s takeover of U.S. Steel and oust the leadership of the U.S. steelmaker after taking a stake in the company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate tees up more confirmations as House GOP talks Trump agenda
item.description: (The Hill) — The Senate is set to churn through more of President Donald Trump’s cabinet nominees this week, with a handful of his controversial picks set to field questions during their confirmation hearings. Tulsi Gabbard, Trump’s pick to be director of national intelligence; Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Alaskans say Trump can change the name of Denali but can't make people call it Mount McKinley
item.description: President Donald Trump has changed the name of Alaska's Denali back to Mount McKinley. It's a nod to President William McKinley, who was from Ohio and who never set foot in Alaska. Many Alaskans say they'll never stop calling the mountain Denali.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nikki Fried vows to rebuild Florida Democrats as re-elected chair
item.description: Nikki Fried was reelected Saturday as chair of the Florida Democratic Party, which is seeking to regain relevance in a state that has tilted decidedly toward the Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Trump's embattled pick for defense secretary, wins Senate confirmation
item.description: Vice President JD Vance cast a tie-breaking vote as Hegseth overcame allegations of sexual assault, public drunkenness and questions of financial mismanagement to win Senate approval.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Measure to make California an independent country cleared to gather signatures
item.description: Another ballot measure asking whether California should be its own country is cleared to start gathering signatures, the California Secretary of State announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he wants to overhaul FEMA as he travels to North Carolina and California
item.description: President Trump is taking the first trip of his term on Friday to North Carolina and California, visiting communities grappling with recovery from natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid and Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: How Trump's gender order impacts Californians' passports, driver's licenses
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order declaring the U.S. only recognizes two genders, male and female, has raised questions about what this means for the validity of documents distributed to transgender Californians with their preferred gender marker.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: House passes Laken Riley Act, sending it to Trump for first legislative win
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed the Laken Riley Act, sending the immigration-related bill to President Trump’s desk in what is poised to be his first legislative victory since returning to the White House this week. The chamber cleared the bill in a 263-156 vote.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Adams says he won't publicly criticize President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams said Tuesday he would not criticize President Donald Trump publicly. Adams also doggedly defended his decision to attend Trump’s inauguration Monday and skip local Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11
